frame_label: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
generated_comment: Bhai practical baat hai, zamaana theek nahi hai. Ladkiyon ko bhi thoda samajhna chahiye ki late night akeli ya random jagah jana avoid karein, apna khayal rakhein — aadmi kab kya kar de, koi bharosa nahi. Baad mein sab system ko bolte hain, par basic precautions bhi zaroori hote hain.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames male violence as inevitable and shifts responsibility onto women to restrict their movement and behavior. It uses a protective, practical tone to justify victim-blaming and safety burdening ('late night akeli', 'basic precautions').
persona_text: You are Deepak. Age 41. Married. A Punjabi man working as a senior manager in Chandigarh. You are in a private WhatsApp chat with two male friends after one of them shared a news article about a female politician who allegedly had an affair with a married colleague. The article has a photo of the woman. You are reacting to the article and the photo and giving your opinion on what kind of woman gets involved with a married man and what her motivations are.
